rulings.Case Title: FORD MOTOR CREDIT COMPANY LLC VS. SAIRA I FREGOSO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 5, 2014, Line 16, PLAINTIFF FORD MOTOR CREDIT COMPANY, LLC'S Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Hearing required. Defendant shall bring the following evidence to the hearing to substantiate her claim that the money in the levied upon bank account belonged to her roommates: (1) evidence of the transfers of funds from her roommates to Defendant and (2) checks (or other proof of payment) from the previous two months showing the amount of rent paid to the landlord. If the rent is not divided equally among the roommates, Defendant is also required to bring evidence that establishes the amount of rent each roommate pays. Additionally, to the extent that Defendant claims the other money levied upon is exempt, Defendant must bring proof that the funds can be traced to her paid earnings. (See CCP sec. 704.070.) =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: GATESMAN + DAVE, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hrg On Petition For Order Enforcing Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 5, 2014, Line 1, PETITIONER GATESMAN + DAVE, INC Hearing On Petition For Order Enforcing Subpoena. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Parties to appear with proposed orders. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BLUE TEE CORP. DBA BROWN-STRAUSS STEEL VS. DIVERSIFIED PRODUCT INDUSTRIES LTD; A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication By Diversified Product Industries, Ltd. Against Barnard Impregilo Healy Joint Venture
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 5, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT DIVERSIFIED PRODUCT INDUSTRIES LTD; A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The motion is denied. First, the moving party failed to provide a courtesy copy of its papers in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Second, the opposing papers indicate that the moving party has stonewalled discovery and that is grounds to deny the motion. (CCP 437c(h) and (i)). Lastly, the moving party is an entity that is improperly proceeding in pro per. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY "SINTEZ" DIRECTLY AND VS. ALEXANDRE BAPA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Petition For Stay Of This Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 5, 2014, Line 12, PLAINTIFF LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY "SINTEZ" DIRECTLY AND DERIVATIVELY ON BEHALF OF SINTEZ, LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Petition For Stay Of This Action. The motion to compel arbitration is granted and the Court stays this action. The broadly phrased arbitration clause applies to the instant matter. Assuming that the court has the power to rule on the unconscionability issue, defendant fails to demonstrate unconscionability. Defendant does not present a declaration or any other evidence showing how the agreement was allegedly foisted upon defendant or that defendant could not understand the terms of the agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
